title: Conflict Stages, Semantic Resolution, and Rerere · Graph Lens: Gita Rerere Record Reuse
summary: A repeated long-running integration conflicts across generated code, renamed policy files, and tests; previous manual resolutions are inconsistent and one clean textual result changes behavior. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita rerere record reuse increment in three-stage conflict and rerere resolution dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Enable, inspect, forget, retrain, and validate rerere preimages and postimages while understanding automatic staging and retention.
Reconstruct intent from base and both histories, produce a semantic combined result, and test invariants that neither side tested alone.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook

content.code:
# Worked focus: gita-rerere-record-reuse — Enable, inspect, forget, retrain, and validate rerere preimages and postimages while understanding automatic staging and retention.
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 2
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: the clean-looking file hides a stage-level rename and content conflict
git ls-files --unmerged
# expected: stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, and stage 3 theirs identify exact objects for reconstruction
# observation: all three policy blobs differ and the base path proves the rename context
# changed-case: reuse a recorded resolution on a changed conflict and require the combined behavior test to fail
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: record MERGE_HEAD and both branch tips before aborting or continuing the sequencer
# transfer-limit: rerere output remains a proposal and must pass semantic changed-case verification

steps.title: Build Gita Rerere Record Reuse evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-rerere-record-reuse-865p1i" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-rerere-record-reuse-865p1i
# scenario-key: trace-865p1i probe-i1p568 boundary-5p1i86 owner-865p1ix fault-865p1iz
# before-ref: main=a11ce86 candidate=b00b11i
# hypothesis: the clean-looking file hides a stage-level rename and content conflict
git ls-files --unmerged
# expected: stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, and stage 3 theirs identify exact objects for reconstruction
# observation: all three policy blobs differ and the base path proves the rename context
# changed-case: reuse a recorded resolution on a changed conflict and require the combined behavior test to fail
# after-ref: main=a11ce86 candidate=c0ffeei
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: record MERGE_HEAD and both branch tips before aborting or continuing the sequencer
# transfer-limit: rerere output remains a proposal and must pass semantic changed-case verification
